steps.title: Limit the bumblebee model
steps.content: The passage says bumblebees are heavily studied, so researchers use them to understand wild-bee declines. But Moreau notes that bumblebees have resilience traits that many other wild bees lack, so conclusions drawn from bumblebees may not always transfer.

steps.title: Keep the caution precise
steps.content: Choice A wrongly suggests bumblebees are unlikely to experience major population changes. Choice B rejects using bumblebees too broadly, even for similar bees. Choice C invents a ranking of climate variation and habitat destruction. The remaining choice captures the need for caution when extrapolating from bumblebees. The answer is D.
